title: Overview
intro:
Welcome. This site publishes a dataset and lets **anyone analyze it in plain language** — type a question and an AI agent writes and runs real Python (pandas, matplotlib) against the data, then posts the resulting chart, table, and a written summary to a shared gallery.

This is the demo content that ships with the template. **You edit `content/sections.json`** (this file), then run `npm run content` to regenerate `web/public/sections.json`. Each entry becomes a section in the left-hand outline. A section with `"id": "raw"` renders the interactive data table; others show their `intro` (Markdown, with `$LaTeX$` math).

title: The dataset
intro: Browse the data below — sort, filter, and read each column's description. Download the full CSV, or open the **Analyze** tab to ask the agent a question about it.

title: Published results
intro:
Drop your own Stata output here. Export tables as LaTeX (`esttab ... using file.tex`) into `web/public/stata/tab/` and figures (PNG/SVG) into `web/public/stata/fig/`, then reference them by name below. Tables render live from their LaTeX; figures show as images. Run `npm run content` to rebuild.

The two items below are examples that ship with the template:

analyses.writeup: Example regression table (rendered from `example_regression.tex`). The treatment effect is **3.84** (SE 1.40), significant at the 5% level.
Example figure (`example_outcome_by_group.svg`): mean outcome by group.

title: Run your own analysis
intro: Open the **Analyze** tab, enter your name, and describe an analysis in plain language (for example: *“compare the outcome between the treatment and control groups and test whether the difference is significant”*). The agent writes an `analysis.py`, runs it against the data in an isolated workspace, and returns its figures, tables, and a plain-language summary. You can edit the script and re-run it, or publish the result to the shared **Gallery**.
